English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Community, Food and Agriculture Program records
Cornell University. Community, Food & Agriculture Program
Community, Food & Agriculture Program
Cornell Farming Alternatives Program
21-46-3763
27 cubic feet. 27 cubic feet.
1960-2000. Correspondence, Publications, Reports Includes correspondence, publications, reports and memos about the program. Collection material in English 6684287
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
G. BaconC. HillA. Shah
Date completed:
May 2010
EAD encoding:
Evan Earle, July 2010
Date modified:
RMC Staff, July 2011
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
The Community, Food & Agriculture Program (CFAP) was launched as the Cornell Farming Alterntatives Program in 1986, which focused on research, training and outreach materials designed to help struggling dairy farmers transition to more diverse and profitable agricultural enterprises. In 2002 the program changed its name to CFAP and remained committed to the mission of sustaining farms and communities in New York State and the Northeast. The program ended in 2006.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes correspondence, publications, reports and memos about the program.
Cite As:
Community, Food & Agriculture Program records, #21-46-3763. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Rural development. Agriculture -- United States. Sustainable Agriculture -- New York (State). Agriculture -- New York (State) -- Periodicals. Dairying. Farmers -- Economic conditions.
